description_long: This is a single-propeller, Soviet-era fighter airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a distinctive red nose and engine cowling. The aircraft features a low-mounted, straight wing configuration with rounded wingtips and a taildragger landing gear setup. The fuselage is painted light gray with red Soviet stars and the number '12' on the sides and tail, and the cockpit is enclosed with a clear, bubble-style canopy. The propeller is mounted at the front, and the engine exhausts are visible along the sides of the nose. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both with red star insignia. The overall design suggests a World War II-era military aircraft, likely constructed primarily from metal alloys.
description: A Soviet-style, single-propeller fighter airplane with a red nose, gray fuselage, and red star insignia, featuring a low-wing and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the red propeller nose, bubble canopy, and left wing with landing gear extended.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section, red star insignia, and the number '12' on the fuselage and tail.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the right wing, tail, and the aircraft's overall streamlined shape.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the propeller, engine exhausts, and cockpit canopy.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with rounded tips and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine, nose-mounted with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a bubble canopy.
